name: azure-eventhub-java
description: Build real-time streaming applications with Azure Event Hubs SDK for Java. Use when implementing event streaming, high-throughput data ingestion, or building event-driven architectures.
capabilities: Create EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient for sending and receiving events
Send single events and event batches with partition keys for ordering
Use EventProcessorClient for production-scale event processing with checkpointing
Handle async event production and consumption with reactive streams
Configure event positions (earliest, latest, offset, sequence, time-based)
Implement error handling for transient AMQP errors
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for Azure Event Hubs Java SDK. The external_commands alerts (38 occurrences) are triggered by backticks in markdown code blocks - these are code examples, not shell commands. The weak_crypto and system_reconnaissance alerts are misinterpretations of documentation text (partition keys, checkpointing). No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Build real-time streaming applications with Azure Event Hubs SDK for Java. Use for event streaming, high-throughput data ingestion, and event-driven architectures.
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Event Hubs namespace configuration
Connection strings and credentials must be managed securely
Does not include sample credentials or test data
User must provide their own Azure resources and authentication

useCases.title: Build event streaming pipelines
useCases.description: Create event producers and consumers for real-time data streaming between systems

useCases.title: Implement high-throughput data ingestion
useCases.description: Handle millions of events per second with batch processing and partition strategies

useCases.title: Create scalable microservices communication
useCases.description: Use Event Hubs as message backbone for microservices event-driven communication

promptTemplates.title: Basic Event Producer
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create an EventHubProducerClient using a connection string in Java

promptTemplates.title: Event Consumer Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create an EventHubConsumerClient to receive events from a specific partition?

promptTemplates.title: Production Event Processor
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an EventProcessorClient with BlobCheckpointStore for production-scale processing

promptTemplates.title: Async Event Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use EventHubProducerAsyncClient for non-blocking event sending

bestPractices: Use EventProcessorClient for production workloads to get automatic load balancing and checkpointing
Batch events using EventDataBatch for efficient network utilization
Always close clients using try-with-resources to ensure proper cleanup
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode connection strings in source code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Avoid sending events one at a time - use batching for throughput
Do not skip checkpointing - this leads to reprocessing events on restart

faq.question: What is the difference between EventHubProducerClient and EventHubProducerAsyncClient?
faq.answer: EventHubProducerClient is synchronous, blocking until operations complete. EventHubProducerAsyncClient is non-blocking and returns Mono/Flux for reactive programming patterns.

faq.question: How do I choose a partition strategy?
faq.answer: Use partition keys for ordering within a specific category (like customer ID). Let Azure assign partitions for even distribution when ordering is not critical.

faq.question: What is checkpointing and why is it important?
faq.answer: Checkpointing saves the position of processed events in Azure Blob Storage. This allows the processor to resume from where it left off after restarts or failures.

faq.question: How do I handle transient errors?
faq.answer: Use the processError callback to catch AmqpException errors. Check isTransient() to identify errors that will be retried automatically.

faq.question: Can I use managed identity instead of connection strings?
faq.answer: Yes, use DefaultAzureCredentialBuilder to authenticate with Azure Active Directory managed identities. This is recommended for production.

faq.question: What is the difference between earliest and latest event position?
faq.answer: earliest() starts from the first available event in the partition. latest() starts from only new events arriving after the consumer connects.
